sections.paragraph:
(1) i.q. זָלַל to pour out, once, Isa 46:6, הַזָּלִיס זָחָב מִכִּיס “pouring out (i.e. lavishing) gold from the bag.” (Arab. ذَالَ IV. to make light of.)

(2) to remove, to take away, compare Arab. ذَالَ Med. Waw and Ye, to take away; intransitively, i.q. to go away, to desist, to fail. Hence זוּלָה.

[Hiphil הִזִּיל for הֵזִיל (comp. the roots סוּת, נוּחַ, לוּן, מוּל, and Gesen. Gram. § 71, note 9), to make light of, to despise, comp. Kal. No. 1. Lam 1:8. Thes.]
